intro: Ozothamnus bidwillii, also known as Bidwill's Ozothamnus or Bidwill's white everlasting daisy, is a flowering plant native to Australia, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ozothamnus bidwillii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Bidwill's Ozothamnus is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to soothe skin irritations and reduce fever.
